architecture.summary: SSAE is a context-conditioned sparse autoencoder that operates at the step level of reasoning trajectories. It consists of a context-conditioned encoder (Transformer) that maps concatenated context and step to a dense embedding, a sparse projector that expands this embedding into a high-dimensional sparse latent space with controlled sparsity, and a context-conditioned decoder (Transformer) that reconstructs the step by integrating latent features with contextual embeddings. The key mechanism is an information bottleneck formed by sparsity constraints, which forces the latent vector to encode only incremental information relative to prior context, squeezing out background information. A dynamic weight controller automatically adjusts the sparsity regularization coefficient during training to maintain target sparsity.

biblio.title: Step-Level Sparse Autoencoder for Reasoning Process Interpretation

claims.statement: SSAE features can predict step correctness with at least 10% accuracy increase compared with naive majority-class prediction, achieving 78.58% on GSM8K and 82.74% on MATH-500 for SSAE-Qwen
SSAE features can almost perfectly predict step length with Step Length Error of 2.10 on GSM8K and 1.94 on MATH-500 for SSAE-Qwen

baseline.name: stochastic baseline from training set distribution

claims.statement: SSAE features predict first token perplexity with 4.09 on GSM8K and 1.46 on MATH-500 for SSAE-Qwen, compared to baseline of 61.01 and 74.96 respectively
N2G pattern extraction for SSAE-Qwen achieves F1 score of 79.49 on GSM8K with sparsity target of 10, compared to 61.03 at sparsity 5 and 52.73 at sparsity 3
Probe-Guided weighted voting strategy achieves 46.80% on GSM8K for Qwen2.5-0.5B, compared to Self-Consistency baseline of 46.20%
Latent-swap test shows decoded content semantic independence score of 4.96 out of 5 for SSAE-Qwen on GSM8K with observed agreement of 94.87% and Gwet's AC1 of 0.96 across multiple LLM judges

Token-based SAEs fail to capture step-level information such as reasoning direction and semantic transitions, with performance similar to statistical baselines, whereas SSAE achieves accurate prediction of these properties

SSAE disentangles shared dimensions (encoding surface-level stylistic attributes like lexical choice and syntax) from unique dimensions (encoding deep reasoning-specific attributes like order of operations and reasoning direction)
